stories.title: Mike Tapp leaves role as migration minister weeks after Mahmood row
stories.summary: Mike Tapp has left his role as UK migration minister just weeks after a public dispute with Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood. The junior minister’s exit follows his opposition to her proposed immigration reforms, including calls to exempt care workers from indefinite leave to remain changes. He confirmed his departure on social media, citing loyalty to Prime Minister Keir Starmer. Jo White, chair of the Red Wall Labour MPs, will replace him.

stories.title: Jean-Marc Sylvestre, journaliste économique, est mort à 79 ans
stories.summary: Jean-Marc Sylvestre, journaliste économique français, est mort à 79 ans des suites d’une longue maladie, a annoncé Atlantico le 21 juillet 2020. Figure emblématique de TF1 et LCI, il y a travaillé dix-huit ans, devenant directeur adjoint de l’information avant de rejoindre i-Télé. Collaborateur régulier d’Atlantico pendant quinze ans, il incarnait le libéralisme dans le débat économique. Ses proches et collègues lui rendent hommage.

stories.title: ‘Terrifying’: Woman says landlord was hiding behind wall of Wexford holiday home
stories.summary: A woman and her family were left terrified after finding a landlord hiding behind a wall in their booked holiday home in Co Wexford. Therese, who reserved the entire property via Booking.com for an overnight stay last weekend, said the landlord assured her he wouldn’t be present when they returned. Booking.com confirmed the property has been suspended pending an investigation. Authorities have not yet commented on the incident.

stories.title: Nearly 1,000 people have died from Ebola in DR Congo
stories.summary: Nearly 1,000 people have died from Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo since May, with 999 deaths confirmed and 2,473 cases recorded as of Monday. Two deaths have also been reported in neighboring Uganda. The outbreak, caused by the Bundibugyo strain, remains concentrated in northeastern DRC’s Ituri province. Health officials warn the situation remains critical as cases continue to rise.

stories.title: Las Feministas del PSOE y 96 asociaciones de España y Latinoamérica cargan contra Bibiana Aído ante ONU por atacar a la relatora de Mujer
stories.summary: Bibiana Aído, directora regional de ONU Mujeres para América Latina y el Caribe, enfrenta una denuncia de más de 96 asociaciones feministas de España y Latinoamérica ante el secretario general de la ONU, António Guterres. Las organizaciones acusan a Aído de usar su cargo para atacar a Reem Alsalem, relatora de la ONU sobre violencia contra la mujer, por no alinearse con el feminismo 'queer'. La polémica surge tras un informe de ONU Mujeres Brasil que normaliza conceptos como 'trabajo sexual' y gestación subrogada. La ONU revisará la denuncia mientras crece el debate sobre políticas de género en la región.

stories.title: Casemiro: Inter Miami being investigated for tampering after signing ex-Manchester United midfielder
stories.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after the club signed Brazilian midfielder Casemiro on a deal through 2027 with an option to extend until 2029. The investigation concerns Casemiro's discovery rights, which Inter Miami acquired from the Los Angeles Galaxy. Casemiro, a 34-year-old free agent, joins Inter Miami after a stint with Manchester United. The league will review the matter before commenting further.

stories.title: Le retour du plafond à 1,99 euro le litre: Totalenergies rétablit son bouclier carburant à la veille de l'annonce de résultats qui s'annoncent en très forte hausse
stories.summary: Totalenergies has reinstated its fuel price cap in France, setting the price of gasoline at 1.99 euros per liter and diesel at 2.25 euros per liter in all its service stations nationwide. This move comes as international oil prices surge due to renewed tensions in the Middle East. The cap was previously limited to 1,200 rural stations. The decision is made ahead of the company's expected strong quarterly earnings release. The price cap is set to remain in place for the summer weekend getaway periods.

stories.title: Pesticidi: richiamati riso Carnaroli, riso Roma integrale e kit pasta e fagioli
stories.summary: The Ministry of Health has recalled three products due to pesticide contamination: Tenca Colombo & C.’s Carnaroli rice (lot 13022036) for tetrametrin, Riso Vignola’s Roma integrale rice for banned chlorpyrifos, and a pasta and bean kit for possible glyphosate in dried beans. The Carnaroli rice was produced in Morano sul Po, Alessandria, with a best-before date of March 31, 2027. Retailers including Il Gigante have pulled the Roma integrale rice from shelves. Consumers are advised to return affected products.

stories.title: Obama Takes Shot at Vance Over Marriage ‘Hypocrisy’
stories.summary: Former President Barack Obama criticized Vice President JD Vance for hypocrisy in his stance on immigration, pointing out that Vance's wife is a daughter of Indian immigrants who received US citizenship at birth. Obama made the comments on a podcast discussing MAGA ideas and the Trump administration's push to end birthright citizenship. The issue affects over 400,000 US-born children of immigrants who could lose citizenship rights under the proposed policy. Obama's comments come as the debate over immigration and citizenship continues.

stories.title: The iPhone 18 Pro's Rumored Camera Upgrade, Explained
stories.summary: Apple’s iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max, launching in September 2026, will feature a first-of-its-kind variable-aperture camera. The upgrade promises smoother outdoor video by mechanically adjusting light intake, reducing jerky motion in bright conditions. Production began in July 2026 alongside Apple’s debut foldable iPhone. This follows the iPhone 17 Pro’s 48MP triple-camera system and improved thermal performance.

stories.title: „Spannerkabinen“ auf neuem Kreuzfahrtschiff sorgen für Aufruhr: „Völlige Fehlkonstruktion“
stories.summary: A new TUI Cruises cruise ship, "Mein Schiff Flow", is facing controversy over certain balcony cabins on deck 6, known as "Spannerkabinen" or "Voyeurkabinen", due to a lack of privacy as a public promenade passes directly in front of them. The ship, launched in June 2026, has cabins that offer unobstructed views but also allow passersby to see into the rooms. The issue has sparked heated debates in the travel community, with some warning against booking these cabins. The ship can accommodate up to 3,984 passengers.

stories.title: Ryanair Spain flight suffers 'technical issue' and met by emergency services
stories.summary: A Ryanair flight from Malaga to Edinburgh, FR5090, declared a mid-air emergency and issued a 7700 squawk code before landing safely at Edinburgh Airport on July 22. The crew reported a minor technical issue, and the plane was met by emergency services and inspected by engineers. Passengers disembarked normally. An investigation into the cause of the issue is ongoing.

stories.title: “Un Indien dans la ville”, comédie à succès gentiment satirique
stories.summary: A French comedy film "Un Indien dans la ville" remains a box office success, having attracted eight million viewers in the 1990s. The film tells the story of a stressed businessman who discovers he has a son living with a tribe in the Amazon and takes him to Paris. The movie features a lighthearted and satirical take on cultural clashes, with a cast including Thierry Lhermitte and Patrick Timsit. The film's success led to a US remake and a popular song by Tonton David.
stories.trend: pauline pinsolle un indien dans la ville

stories.title: Lotto 6aus49 am Mittwoch: Das sind die Gewinnzahlen vom 22.7.2026
stories.summary: A 50-million-euro jackpot in the German Lotto 6aus49 was up for grabs on July 22, 2026, with a 1 in 14 million chance of winning. The winning numbers were 3, 5, 10, 14, 25, and 49, with a superzahl of 3. The drawing took place at 18:25 on the same day.

stories.title: Man, 30s, dies after being struck by van in Co Kilkenny
stories.summary: A man in his 30s has died after being struck by a van on the R693 near Arigan Bridge in Co Kilkenny at 9:30am. This is the 101st road fatality in Ireland so far this year. The man, a pedestrian, was pronounced dead at the scene and his body was taken to University Hospital Waterford for a post-mortem examination. Gardaí are appealing for witnesses to come forward with any camera footage or information.

stories.title: Zendaya faces backlash for wearing ancient Iranian jewellery at Odyssey event
stories.summary: American actress Zendaya is facing backlash for wearing ancient Iranian jewellery at a London event promoting the film Odyssey. The earrings, featuring gold plaques from the 1st millennium BC, were created by a London jeweller and purchased from another dealer in 2025. Archaeologists have criticised the use of culturally significant artefacts as accessories, calling it a "hijacking" of Iran's contributions to humanity. The controversy has sparked a broader debate over the ownership and display of antiquities.

stories.title: Beşiktaş'ta Vincenzo Italiano'dan transfer açıklaması! "Üzerinde çalışıyoruz"
stories.summary: Beşiktaş, UEFA Avrupa Ligi ikinci ön eleme turunda Danimarka temsilcisi Midtjylland ile 23 Temmuz Perşembe günü Tüpraş Stadı'nda karşılaşacak. Teknik direktör Vincenzo Italiano, son 10 gündür tam kadroyla çalıştıklarını ve sahaya Beşiktaş kimliğini yansıtan bir ekip çıkarmayı hedeflediklerini belirtti. İtalyan, Avrupa yolculuğunun heyecan verici olduğunu vurgularken, takımın ilk resmi maçında galibiyetle başlamak istediklerini ifade etti.

stories.title: Mid-air mass brawl halts easyJet's Tenerife to Liverpool flight
stories.summary: A mid-air brawl involving around 10 passengers forced an easyJet flight from Tenerife to Liverpool to return to the Spanish island on Tuesday evening. The 186-passenger aircraft, flight EZY3352, was met by police at Tenerife South airport before continuing to its original destination. The captain made the decision to return to Tenerife due to the safety risk posed by the fight. The airline is investigating the incident.

stories.title: World Matchplay 2026: Cameron Menzies receives medical care, Gerwyn Price, James Wade and Gian van Veen win
stories.summary: Cameron Menzies withdrew from the World Matchplay 2026 in Blackpool on Wednesday after collapsing mid-match due to high blood pressure. Gerwyn Price and Gian van Veen advanced to the quarter-finals with victories over Rob Cross and Wessel Nijman respectively. Price leads Smith in their upcoming quarter-final rematch. The tournament continues with defending champion Luke Littler still in contention.

stories.title: Opinion | Amnesty International UK vs. J.K. Rowling
stories.summary: Amnesty International UK has withdrawn a report accusing 117 organizations, including the Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales, of being part of an "anti-rights movement" in the UK. The report claimed these groups spent £144 million between 2019 and 2024, a 47% increase. The backlash was immediate, with Catholic leaders defending their mission as rooted in human dignity. The controversy has reignited a polarizing debate over gender-identity activism and the defense of biological sex.

stories.title: Shownieuws creëert verwarring over lot Greetje in Urk!: 'Doet echt niet meer mee'
stories.summary: Reality TV star Greetje Hakvoort's status on Urk is unclear after conflicting statements on Shownieuws. The show's presenter Fien Vermeulen said Greetje will no longer be in the series, but earlier, host Teun Kapitein stated she is still participating. The show's earlier statement contradicts Fien's claim, saying Greetje will not leave the series. The situation remains uncertain, with Greetje's future in Urk still to be determined.

stories.title: Nexsen Establishes Hong Kong Research Facility to Tap US$5.36 Billion Asia-Pacific Diagnostics Market
stories.summary: Nexsen Limited has established a research and development facility in Hong Kong to tap into the US$5.36 billion Asia-Pacific diagnostics market. The facility will support development and clinical validation of the company's diagnostic technologies, including two new programs targeting traumatic brain injury and neonatal sepsis. The Hong Kong location positions Nexsen closer to Asian healthcare regulators and target populations. The company's expansion aims to pursue regional market entry in the Asia-Pacific region.

stories.title: Even camels can't cope: Africa's ships of the desert hit by rising temperatures
stories.summary: Camel herders in Ethiopia’s Afar region report rising temperatures are killing calves and harming adult camels, with one herder losing eight in a month. The extreme heat causes blisters, burns, and hair loss in the animals, which have long been relied on for survival in the arid landscape. Experts warn the trend mirrors broader climate shifts affecting livestock across North and East Africa. Livestock losses are expected to rise as temperatures climb further.

stories.title: One Morning of Controlled Chaos in Maine
stories.summary: A contentious Maine Senate race has seen a surge in voter engagement, with over 1,000 people attending a caucus meeting in Sanford on Sunday. The event was part of the process to replace Graham Platner, who dropped out of the race. The meeting allowed participants to vote for a group of delegates who will ultimately choose a new candidate. The outcome of the meeting will determine the Democratic nominee for the Maine Senate seat.

stories.title: Klątwa wisi nad "Lewym". Czeka na to już 18 lat!
stories.summary: Polish striker Robert Lewandowski is set to make his debut for Chicago Fire after 18 years of waiting, with the match against Vancouver Whitecaps rescheduled due to poor air quality in Chicago caused by wildfires in Canada. Lewandowski will now face Inter in Miami, marking his first appearance for the team. He has a history of struggling in his debut matches, having never scored in his first game for a new club. The 18-year wait for his debut will come to an end on Thursday night.

stories.title: July 23, 2026: Alcala Gurri vs Dietrich Tennis Prediction Market
stories.summary: Professional tennis player M. Alcala Gurri is favored to win the July 23, 2026 match against D. Dietrich, with odds of 45 cents to Dietrich's 55 cents. The match is part of the 2026 ATP Challenger Zug Round Of 16. The winner will be determined after a ball has been played, and payouts will be made within an hour of the event resolution. The match is scheduled to take place on July 23, 2026.

stories.title: Most sewage spills in England may be illegal, new research suggests
stories.summary: New research suggests that nearly 9 in 10 hours of raw sewage spills in England may be illegal, with over 86% of incidents occurring during non-heavy rain conditions. The study analyzed data from over 350 storm overflow outlets across nine water companies serving over 30 million people. This finding raises concerns about the legitimacy of sewage spills in England. The water companies will now face scrutiny over their compliance with regulations.

stories.title: Hope for thousands of MS patients as 'life-changing' drug now on NHS in England
stories.summary: Up to 5,000 people in England with multiple sclerosis will now benefit from the NHS-approved drug fampridine, which boosts nerve signals to help people walk. The life-changing medication is already available in other parts of the UK. Clinical trials showed nearly 40% of patients experienced improvements in walking speed and duration. This decision could significantly enhance the independence of those affected.

stories.title: Indian police cracked down on 'cockroach' protesters. They went home and made memes about it
stories.summary: Indian police cracked down on a massive anti-government protest in Delhi, with tens of thousands of supporters of the Cockroach Janta Party marching towards parliament on Monday. Police fired tear gas and charged demonstrators with batons, injuring many. The protest, which demanded the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, was one of the largest against Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government in recent years. Demonstrators have since taken to social media to mock the police crackdown with memes.

stories.title: Elliot Page presenta la fidanzata Julia Shiplett: chi è la comica al suo fianco sul red carpet di The Odyssey
stories.summary: Elliot Page's partner Julia Shiplett made a rare public appearance at The Odyssey event, supporting him in a significant moment of his career. Julia Shiplett is a writer, copywriter, actress and comedian, born in Chicago and educated at the University of Wisconsin-Madison. She has appeared in several TV shows and written for prestigious publications, including the New York Times. This appearance marks a rare moment of public visibility for the couple, who typically keep their relationship private.

stories.title: Iris si presenterà al falò nella sesta puntata di “Temptation Island 2026”? Tutte le anticipazioni
stories.summary: Tonight, Filippo Bisciglia and the remaining four couples, including Bernadette and Diamante, will face a crucial decision on Temptation Island 2026. The sixth episode airs at 21:30 on Wednesday, July 22, as the participants navigate their feelings and relationships. Andrea, who has been involved with single temptress Sary, has requested a confrontation with his girlfriend Iris, which she must decide to accept. The outcome will determine the future of their relationship.

stories.title: Airport drop-off fees up by a third - here are the priciest
stories.summary: Drop-off fees at the UK's major airports have increased by a third on average since last summer, with two London airports, Gatwick and Stansted, charging the highest fees at £10. This rise comes as Gatwick aims to reduce car usage and increase train and bus arrivals. The fees are part of airports' business models and climate change objectives. The UK's airports now have some of the highest drop-off fees in Europe.

stories.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The US has signed a landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia, allowing the country to enrich uranium on its soil at a US-built facility. The deal, worth billions of dollars to the US, will be used to produce fuel for power plants. US Secretary of Energy Chris Wright said the agreement upholds the highest standards of nuclear safety and nonproliferation. The deal is expected to advance regional security and strengthen the US's competitive edge in civil nuclear technology.

stories.title: Juve-Vlahovic: prove di rinnovo, ma c'è molta distanza. E Carnevali non molla Kolo Muani
stories.summary: Juventus and striker Dusan Vlahovic have begun negotiations on a contract renewal, with the club offering up to 6 million euros in annual salary and commissions, while Vlahovic is seeking 7 million euros annually and double that for signing. The two sides are still far apart, with a significant gap in their demands. The Juventus is also pursuing French striker Kolo Muani, but faces a major obstacle in securing him from Paris Saint-Germain, which has already rejected three offers from the Italian club. The negotiations are expected to be a long and challenging process.

stories.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
stories.summary: Ukrainian drones have struck two Russian warehouses belonging to online retailer Wildberries, killing seven workers and injuring dozens in the Tambov region and near Moscow. The attacks, which also damaged goods worth an estimated £750m, are part of Kyiv's campaign to disrupt Russian military supplies. Hundreds of Russian sellers who rely on Wildberries for their livelihoods are now facing financial losses. The strikes mark a new escalation in Ukraine's war against Russia.

stories.title: McInnes glad of Rangers backing in appeal against ban
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been given a four-match touchline ban by the Scottish FA for criticizing a late penalty decision in a May match against Motherwell. The ban is an immediate three-game suspension with one game pending until the end of the season. McInnes' comments were made when he was in charge of Hearts, and Rangers are now appealing the decision. The case will likely be a key focus in the coming weeks.

stories.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
stories.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet reported a negative free cash flow of $5.9 billion for the first time in at least a decade, largely due to spiraling costs of artificial intelligence infrastructure. The company's AI spending is expected to reach $205 billion this year, a significant increase from last year. This surge in spending comes as major tech companies invest heavily in AI technology. Alphabet's stock fell 4% in after-hours trading following the financial report.

stories.title: Yeon Sang-ho Returns to ‘Parasyte’ Universe With ‘Tamiya,’ Starring Shioli Kutsuna, at Netflix
stories.summary: South Korean filmmaker Yeon Sang-ho is returning to the "Parasyte" universe with a new live-action series, "Parasyte: Tamiya," starring Shioli Kutsuna, set to stream on Netflix in 2027. The series marks a collaboration between Netflix and Yeon, who developed the show and co-wrote the script with Ryu Yong-jae. This is Yeon's second project with Netflix, following "Hellbound." The series is expected to premiere next year.

stories.title: Conservatives Parrot A Victimhood Script Written By Barack Obama
stories.summary: Former US President Donald Trump is credited with winning the 2024 presidential election, despite being seen as a foe of socialism, with some attributing his victory to the influence of social media platform TikTok. According to reports, Trump's campaign benefited from the platform's reach, which also fueled opposition to the war in Iran. The election took place on a specific date, with Trump's win marking a significant outcome. Trump's presidency was marked by controversy, including the Iran invasion.

stories.title: Christmas Day Carvery at Rutland Hall Returns for 2026
stories.summary: Rutland Hall Hotel & Spa is hosting a Christmas Day Carvery on December 25, 2026, in The Pavilion, featuring traditional festive food and classic Christmas trimmings. The event is priced at £95 per adult and £47.50 per child, with early booking recommended. This marks the return of a popular Christmas Day experience from last year. Guests can also look forward to a Boxing Day Gala Dinner on December 26, 2026.

stories.title: 'Bad Times at the El Royale' bei Pro7:....im TV
stories.summary: A TV broadcast of the 2018 film "Bad Times at the El Royale" by Drew Goddard aired on July 23, 2026, on Pro7. The movie is available for streaming on Pro7's online platform, but it will not be rebroadcast on linear TV. The film follows a group of guests with different backgrounds who check into the El Royale hotel, only to discover they are being watched. The guests must navigate their complex situations, leading to a tense and suspenseful outcome.
stories.trend: bad times at the el royale

stories.title: talkSPORT tips – Best football bets and expert advice for Wednesday 22 July
stories.summary: Major League Soccer resumes after a two-month break with several matches on Wednesday, including New England Revolution hosting Toronto. The Revolution are favored to win, with Toronto having kept just two clean sheets all season. The match is part of a busy schedule with qualifiers in European competitions also taking place. The outcome of this match will be a key indicator of Toronto's performance after a nine-match winless run in MLS.

stories.title: World Cup 2026: Gavi does not think Paredes should be suspended
stories.summary: Spain midfielder Gavi believes Argentina's Leandro Paredes should not be suspended for his role in a confrontation following the World Cup final, where Paredes appeared to push Eric Garcia by the throat. The incident occurred as Spain celebrated their 1-0 win. A disciplinary and ethics prosecutor has been appointed to assess the situation, with potential suspensions and fines possible. The Argentine Football Association may also face a fine.

stories.title: Phillies vs. Dodgers Preview: Odds, Pitching Matchup, and Props for July 22
stories.summary: The Philadelphia Phillies host the Los Angeles Dodgers at Citizens Bank Park on July 22, 2026, with first pitch at 6:40 p.m. ET. The Phillies aim to bounce back from a 2-1 defeat, seeking to prove their mettle against a top-tier opponent. They trail the Atlanta Braves by three games in the NL East standings, with a 56-46 record. The Dodgers, 64-38, look to build momentum after a 3-2 record in their last five contests.

stories.title: Tears and sleepless nights as Marshall and Adlington get Scotland flag honour
stories.summary: Scotland’s bowls team will carry the national flag at the Games, led by 59-year-old East Lothian bowler David Marshall and judoka and three-time gold medalist Emma Adlington. Marshall, joined by his niece Beth Riva, called the honour emotional and admitted he nearly cried when told. Adlington, aiming for a third straight gold, revealed the secret had been kept for weeks. The opening ceremony takes place on Friday.

stories.title: Inter Miami vs Chicago Fire live online: score, stats, goals & updates | MLS 2026
stories.summary: In a highly contested MLS match, Inter Miami CF took the lead in the 9th minute after a handball by Chicago Fire FC's Anton Salétros. The free kick was awarded to Inter Miami's Sergio Reguilón, who scored the goal. The match is currently underway with Inter Miami holding a 1-0 advantage. The outcome of the game will determine the standings in the MLS 2026 season.

stories.title: Liverpool confident of signing Barcola - Thursday's gossip
stories.summary: Liverpool is confident of signing Paris St-Germain forward Bradley Barcola, a 23-year-old who impressed with four goal contributions at the World Cup with France this summer. The transfer would come after beating Arsenal and Bayern Munich to the signature. Barcola's World Cup performance has boosted Liverpool's chances of securing the young forward. Liverpool will now focus on finalizing the deal.

stories.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant in response to ongoing attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz. The potential targets include those located near the capital Tehran. This warning comes after 11 consecutive nights of US strikes on Iran and Iranian state media reports of air defences activated in Tehran. The US has yet to confirm any specific action.

stories.title: Shaken, Soaked and Seasick: What It’s Like to Survive ‘The Odyssey’ in 4DX
stories.summary: A new immersive screening of Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey in 4DX adds seat rumbling, wind, and water effects to the three-hour epic. The experience, available at select theaters, targets fans seeking heightened sensory thrills beyond standard formats. Showtimes begin this weekend, offering a fresh way to experience the Matt Damon-starring adventure.

stories.title: ‘Doctor Strange’ Director Says Elon Musk ‘Doesn’t Know S— About Cinema’ After Musk Vows to Make AI ‘Odyssey’: ‘His Opinions on Art Are Worthless’
stories.summary: Elon Musk vowed on July 22, 2026, to use his AI tool Grok to produce a full-length, historically accurate film adaptation of Homer’s The Odyssey by year’s end. Director Scott Derrickson, known for Doctor Strange, publicly dismissed Musk’s claim, calling him uninformed about cinema and art. Musk had previously criticized Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey over casting choices. The debate highlights tensions between AI-driven filmmaking and traditional Hollywood perspectives.

stories.title: A year after deadly jet crash at Bangladesh school, families demand answers
stories.summary: A year after a Bangladeshi air force jet crashed into a school in Dhaka, killing 36 people, mostly children, families and survivors are still seeking answers. The deadly incident on July 23, 2022, injured over 160 people. Families of the victims gathered at a hospital in Dhaka on Tuesday to mark the anniversary, with many students struggling to return to normal life. The investigation into the crash is ongoing.

stories.title: Meta won’t have to face the next planned social media addiction trial
stories.summary: A 15-year-old plaintiff from Florida has dropped a lawsuit against Meta, just days before a trial was set to begin in a Los Angeles courtroom. The case, which was part of a series of bellwether trials, accused Meta of creating features that harmed and hooked teens. Meta will avoid a potential settlement, following the lead of other social media companies like TikTok, Snap, and YouTube, which previously settled similar claims. The plaintiff will now focus on his recovery and therapy.

stories.title: With Saudi Deal, Trump Loosens Nuclear Standards for One Close Ally
stories.summary: Nuclear experts say it throws fuel on the smoldering fire of atomic proliferation, creating a destabilizing situation that could raise the risk of nuclear war.

stories.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
stories.summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

stories.title: Alphabet Quadruples Profit to $112 Billion, Fueled by A.I. Investments
stories.summary: Google’s parent company, which has spent heavily on artificial intelligence, also showed growth across its businesses, particularly in cloud computing.

stories.title: Three Airports Plan to Ditch T.S.A. Amid Push for Private Security
stories.summary: The airports have opted in to the agency’s new security model, an agency spokesperson confirmed on Wednesday. A union official said the move could jeopardize public safety.

stories.title: 1,700 H.I.V. Treatment Sites Closed After Trump Aid Cuts, a Study Finds
stories.summary: A study by amfAR found 1,700 H.I.V. treatment sites closed after U.S. funding cuts last year, slashing prevention and care for high-risk groups. Three-quarters of affected organizations stopped serving sex workers, men who have sex with men, transgender people and people who inject drugs. The findings, presented at an AIDS conference in Rio de Janeiro, contradict earlier State Department claims of stable treatment numbers. Global health advocates warn the cuts could reverse years of progress against the disease.

stories.title: What Were the Lotus-Eaters Eating in Homer’s ‘Odyssey’?
stories.summary: In Homer’s epic, Odysseus’s men eat something so powerful that they forget about home. Which plants could have produced such a soporific, narcotic and amnesiac effect?

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report finds that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a result of personal willpower. The report highlights that ultra-processed foods high in sugar and refined carbohydrates are primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. According to the report, nearly 70% of packaged foods in Britain are ultra-processed. The committee recommends targeting these foods to reduce obesity rates in the country.

offbeat.title: Mike Tapp leaves role as migration minister weeks after Mahmood row
offbeat.summary: Mike Tapp has left his role as UK migration minister just weeks after a public dispute with Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood. The junior minister’s exit follows his opposition to her proposed immigration reforms, including calls to exempt care workers from indefinite leave to remain changes. He confirmed his departure on social media, citing loyalty to Prime Minister Keir Starmer. Jo White, chair of the Red Wall Labour MPs, will replace him.

offbeat.title: Jean-Marc Sylvestre, journaliste économique, est mort à 79 ans
offbeat.summary: Jean-Marc Sylvestre, journaliste économique français, est mort à 79 ans des suites d’une longue maladie, a annoncé Atlantico le 21 juillet 2020. Figure emblématique de TF1 et LCI, il y a travaillé dix-huit ans, devenant directeur adjoint de l’information avant de rejoindre i-Télé. Collaborateur régulier d’Atlantico pendant quinze ans, il incarnait le libéralisme dans le débat économique. Ses proches et collègues lui rendent hommage.

offbeat.title: ‘Terrifying’: Woman says landlord was hiding behind wall of Wexford holiday home
offbeat.summary: A woman and her family were left terrified after finding a landlord hiding behind a wall in their booked holiday home in Co Wexford. Therese, who reserved the entire property via Booking.com for an overnight stay last weekend, said the landlord assured her he wouldn’t be present when they returned. Booking.com confirmed the property has been suspended pending an investigation. Authorities have not yet commented on the incident.

offbeat.title: Nearly 1,000 people have died from Ebola in DR Congo
offbeat.summary: Nearly 1,000 people have died from Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo since May, with 999 deaths confirmed and 2,473 cases recorded as of Monday. Two deaths have also been reported in neighboring Uganda. The outbreak, caused by the Bundibugyo strain, remains concentrated in northeastern DRC’s Ituri province. Health officials warn the situation remains critical as cases continue to rise.

offbeat.title: Las Feministas del PSOE y 96 asociaciones de España y Latinoamérica cargan contra Bibiana Aído ante ONU por atacar a la relatora de Mujer
offbeat.summary: Bibiana Aído, directora regional de ONU Mujeres para América Latina y el Caribe, enfrenta una denuncia de más de 96 asociaciones feministas de España y Latinoamérica ante el secretario general de la ONU, António Guterres. Las organizaciones acusan a Aído de usar su cargo para atacar a Reem Alsalem, relatora de la ONU sobre violencia contra la mujer, por no alinearse con el feminismo 'queer'. La polémica surge tras un informe de ONU Mujeres Brasil que normaliza conceptos como 'trabajo sexual' y gestación subrogada. La ONU revisará la denuncia mientras crece el debate sobre políticas de género en la región.

offbeat.title: Casemiro: Inter Miami being investigated for tampering after signing ex-Manchester United midfielder
offbeat.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after the club signed Brazilian midfielder Casemiro on a deal through 2027 with an option to extend until 2029. The investigation concerns Casemiro's discovery rights, which Inter Miami acquired from the Los Angeles Galaxy. Casemiro, a 34-year-old free agent, joins Inter Miami after a stint with Manchester United. The league will review the matter before commenting further.

offbeat.title: Le retour du plafond à 1,99 euro le litre: Totalenergies rétablit son bouclier carburant à la veille de l'annonce de résultats qui s'annoncent en très forte hausse
offbeat.summary: Totalenergies has reinstated its fuel price cap in France, setting the price of gasoline at 1.99 euros per liter and diesel at 2.25 euros per liter in all its service stations nationwide. This move comes as international oil prices surge due to renewed tensions in the Middle East. The cap was previously limited to 1,200 rural stations. The decision is made ahead of the company's expected strong quarterly earnings release. The price cap is set to remain in place for the summer weekend getaway periods.

offbeat.title: Pesticidi: richiamati riso Carnaroli, riso Roma integrale e kit pasta e fagioli
offbeat.summary: The Ministry of Health has recalled three products due to pesticide contamination: Tenca Colombo & C.’s Carnaroli rice (lot 13022036) for tetrametrin, Riso Vignola’s Roma integrale rice for banned chlorpyrifos, and a pasta and bean kit for possible glyphosate in dried beans. The Carnaroli rice was produced in Morano sul Po, Alessandria, with a best-before date of March 31, 2027. Retailers including Il Gigante have pulled the Roma integrale rice from shelves. Consumers are advised to return affected products.

offbeat.title: Obama Takes Shot at Vance Over Marriage ‘Hypocrisy’
offbeat.summary: Former President Barack Obama criticized Vice President JD Vance for hypocrisy in his stance on immigration, pointing out that Vance's wife is a daughter of Indian immigrants who received US citizenship at birth. Obama made the comments on a podcast discussing MAGA ideas and the Trump administration's push to end birthright citizenship. The issue affects over 400,000 US-born children of immigrants who could lose citizenship rights under the proposed policy. Obama's comments come as the debate over immigration and citizenship continues.

offbeat.title: The iPhone 18 Pro's Rumored Camera Upgrade, Explained
offbeat.summary: Apple’s iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max, launching in September 2026, will feature a first-of-its-kind variable-aperture camera. The upgrade promises smoother outdoor video by mechanically adjusting light intake, reducing jerky motion in bright conditions. Production began in July 2026 alongside Apple’s debut foldable iPhone. This follows the iPhone 17 Pro’s 48MP triple-camera system and improved thermal performance.

offbeat.title: „Spannerkabinen“ auf neuem Kreuzfahrtschiff sorgen für Aufruhr: „Völlige Fehlkonstruktion“
offbeat.summary: A new TUI Cruises cruise ship, "Mein Schiff Flow", is facing controversy over certain balcony cabins on deck 6, known as "Spannerkabinen" or "Voyeurkabinen", due to a lack of privacy as a public promenade passes directly in front of them. The ship, launched in June 2026, has cabins that offer unobstructed views but also allow passersby to see into the rooms. The issue has sparked heated debates in the travel community, with some warning against booking these cabins. The ship can accommodate up to 3,984 passengers.

offbeat.title: Ryanair Spain flight suffers 'technical issue' and met by emergency services
offbeat.summary: A Ryanair flight from Malaga to Edinburgh, FR5090, declared a mid-air emergency and issued a 7700 squawk code before landing safely at Edinburgh Airport on July 22. The crew reported a minor technical issue, and the plane was met by emergency services and inspected by engineers. Passengers disembarked normally. An investigation into the cause of the issue is ongoing.

offbeat.title: “Un Indien dans la ville”, comédie à succès gentiment satirique
offbeat.summary: A French comedy film "Un Indien dans la ville" remains a box office success, having attracted eight million viewers in the 1990s. The film tells the story of a stressed businessman who discovers he has a son living with a tribe in the Amazon and takes him to Paris. The movie features a lighthearted and satirical take on cultural clashes, with a cast including Thierry Lhermitte and Patrick Timsit. The film's success led to a US remake and a popular song by Tonton David.
offbeat.trend: pauline pinsolle un indien dans la ville

offbeat.title: Lotto 6aus49 am Mittwoch: Das sind die Gewinnzahlen vom 22.7.2026
offbeat.summary: A 50-million-euro jackpot in the German Lotto 6aus49 was up for grabs on July 22, 2026, with a 1 in 14 million chance of winning. The winning numbers were 3, 5, 10, 14, 25, and 49, with a superzahl of 3. The drawing took place at 18:25 on the same day.

offbeat.title: Man, 30s, dies after being struck by van in Co Kilkenny
offbeat.summary: A man in his 30s has died after being struck by a van on the R693 near Arigan Bridge in Co Kilkenny at 9:30am. This is the 101st road fatality in Ireland so far this year. The man, a pedestrian, was pronounced dead at the scene and his body was taken to University Hospital Waterford for a post-mortem examination. Gardaí are appealing for witnesses to come forward with any camera footage or information.

offbeat.title: Zendaya faces backlash for wearing ancient Iranian jewellery at Odyssey event
offbeat.summary: American actress Zendaya is facing backlash for wearing ancient Iranian jewellery at a London event promoting the film Odyssey. The earrings, featuring gold plaques from the 1st millennium BC, were created by a London jeweller and purchased from another dealer in 2025. Archaeologists have criticised the use of culturally significant artefacts as accessories, calling it a "hijacking" of Iran's contributions to humanity. The controversy has sparked a broader debate over the ownership and display of antiquities.

offbeat.title: Beşiktaş'ta Vincenzo Italiano'dan transfer açıklaması! "Üzerinde çalışıyoruz"
offbeat.summary: Beşiktaş, UEFA Avrupa Ligi ikinci ön eleme turunda Danimarka temsilcisi Midtjylland ile 23 Temmuz Perşembe günü Tüpraş Stadı'nda karşılaşacak. Teknik direktör Vincenzo Italiano, son 10 gündür tam kadroyla çalıştıklarını ve sahaya Beşiktaş kimliğini yansıtan bir ekip çıkarmayı hedeflediklerini belirtti. İtalyan, Avrupa yolculuğunun heyecan verici olduğunu vurgularken, takımın ilk resmi maçında galibiyetle başlamak istediklerini ifade etti.

offbeat.title: Mid-air mass brawl halts easyJet's Tenerife to Liverpool flight
offbeat.summary: A mid-air brawl involving around 10 passengers forced an easyJet flight from Tenerife to Liverpool to return to the Spanish island on Tuesday evening. The 186-passenger aircraft, flight EZY3352, was met by police at Tenerife South airport before continuing to its original destination. The captain made the decision to return to Tenerife due to the safety risk posed by the fight. The airline is investigating the incident.

offbeat.title: World Matchplay 2026: Cameron Menzies receives medical care, Gerwyn Price, James Wade and Gian van Veen win
offbeat.summary: Cameron Menzies withdrew from the World Matchplay 2026 in Blackpool on Wednesday after collapsing mid-match due to high blood pressure. Gerwyn Price and Gian van Veen advanced to the quarter-finals with victories over Rob Cross and Wessel Nijman respectively. Price leads Smith in their upcoming quarter-final rematch. The tournament continues with defending champion Luke Littler still in contention.

offbeat.title: Opinion | Amnesty International UK vs. J.K. Rowling
offbeat.summary: Amnesty International UK has withdrawn a report accusing 117 organizations, including the Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales, of being part of an "anti-rights movement" in the UK. The report claimed these groups spent £144 million between 2019 and 2024, a 47% increase. The backlash was immediate, with Catholic leaders defending their mission as rooted in human dignity. The controversy has reignited a polarizing debate over gender-identity activism and the defense of biological sex.

offbeat.title: Shownieuws creëert verwarring over lot Greetje in Urk!: 'Doet echt niet meer mee'
offbeat.summary: Reality TV star Greetje Hakvoort's status on Urk is unclear after conflicting statements on Shownieuws. The show's presenter Fien Vermeulen said Greetje will no longer be in the series, but earlier, host Teun Kapitein stated she is still participating. The show's earlier statement contradicts Fien's claim, saying Greetje will not leave the series. The situation remains uncertain, with Greetje's future in Urk still to be determined.

offbeat.title: Nexsen Establishes Hong Kong Research Facility to Tap US$5.36 Billion Asia-Pacific Diagnostics Market
offbeat.summary: Nexsen Limited has established a research and development facility in Hong Kong to tap into the US$5.36 billion Asia-Pacific diagnostics market. The facility will support development and clinical validation of the company's diagnostic technologies, including two new programs targeting traumatic brain injury and neonatal sepsis. The Hong Kong location positions Nexsen closer to Asian healthcare regulators and target populations. The company's expansion aims to pursue regional market entry in the Asia-Pacific region.

offbeat.title: One Morning of Controlled Chaos in Maine
offbeat.summary: A contentious Maine Senate race has seen a surge in voter engagement, with over 1,000 people attending a caucus meeting in Sanford on Sunday. The event was part of the process to replace Graham Platner, who dropped out of the race. The meeting allowed participants to vote for a group of delegates who will ultimately choose a new candidate. The outcome of the meeting will determine the Democratic nominee for the Maine Senate seat.

offbeat.title: Klątwa wisi nad "Lewym". Czeka na to już 18 lat!
offbeat.summary: Polish striker Robert Lewandowski is set to make his debut for Chicago Fire after 18 years of waiting, with the match against Vancouver Whitecaps rescheduled due to poor air quality in Chicago caused by wildfires in Canada. Lewandowski will now face Inter in Miami, marking his first appearance for the team. He has a history of struggling in his debut matches, having never scored in his first game for a new club. The 18-year wait for his debut will come to an end on Thursday night.

offbeat.title: July 23, 2026: Alcala Gurri vs Dietrich Tennis Prediction Market
offbeat.summary: Professional tennis player M. Alcala Gurri is favored to win the July 23, 2026 match against D. Dietrich, with odds of 45 cents to Dietrich's 55 cents. The match is part of the 2026 ATP Challenger Zug Round Of 16. The winner will be determined after a ball has been played, and payouts will be made within an hour of the event resolution. The match is scheduled to take place on July 23, 2026.

offbeat.title: Indian police cracked down on 'cockroach' protesters. They went home and made memes about it
offbeat.summary: Indian police cracked down on a massive anti-government protest in Delhi, with tens of thousands of supporters of the Cockroach Janta Party marching towards parliament on Monday. Police fired tear gas and charged demonstrators with batons, injuring many. The protest, which demanded the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, was one of the largest against Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government in recent years. Demonstrators have since taken to social media to mock the police crackdown with memes.

offbeat.title: Elliot Page presenta la fidanzata Julia Shiplett: chi è la comica al suo fianco sul red carpet di The Odyssey
offbeat.summary: Elliot Page's partner Julia Shiplett made a rare public appearance at The Odyssey event, supporting him in a significant moment of his career. Julia Shiplett is a writer, copywriter, actress and comedian, born in Chicago and educated at the University of Wisconsin-Madison. She has appeared in several TV shows and written for prestigious publications, including the New York Times. This appearance marks a rare moment of public visibility for the couple, who typically keep their relationship private.

offbeat.title: Iris si presenterà al falò nella sesta puntata di “Temptation Island 2026”? Tutte le anticipazioni
offbeat.summary: Tonight, Filippo Bisciglia and the remaining four couples, including Bernadette and Diamante, will face a crucial decision on Temptation Island 2026. The sixth episode airs at 21:30 on Wednesday, July 22, as the participants navigate their feelings and relationships. Andrea, who has been involved with single temptress Sary, has requested a confrontation with his girlfriend Iris, which she must decide to accept. The outcome will determine the future of their relationship.

offbeat.title: Juve-Vlahovic: prove di rinnovo, ma c'è molta distanza. E Carnevali non molla Kolo Muani
offbeat.summary: Juventus and striker Dusan Vlahovic have begun negotiations on a contract renewal, with the club offering up to 6 million euros in annual salary and commissions, while Vlahovic is seeking 7 million euros annually and double that for signing. The two sides are still far apart, with a significant gap in their demands. The Juventus is also pursuing French striker Kolo Muani, but faces a major obstacle in securing him from Paris Saint-Germain, which has already rejected three offers from the Italian club. The negotiations are expected to be a long and challenging process.

offbeat.title: Conservatives Parrot A Victimhood Script Written By Barack Obama
offbeat.summary: Former US President Donald Trump is credited with winning the 2024 presidential election, despite being seen as a foe of socialism, with some attributing his victory to the influence of social media platform TikTok. According to reports, Trump's campaign benefited from the platform's reach, which also fueled opposition to the war in Iran. The election took place on a specific date, with Trump's win marking a significant outcome. Trump's presidency was marked by controversy, including the Iran invasion.

offbeat.title: Christmas Day Carvery at Rutland Hall Returns for 2026
offbeat.summary: Rutland Hall Hotel & Spa is hosting a Christmas Day Carvery on December 25, 2026, in The Pavilion, featuring traditional festive food and classic Christmas trimmings. The event is priced at £95 per adult and £47.50 per child, with early booking recommended. This marks the return of a popular Christmas Day experience from last year. Guests can also look forward to a Boxing Day Gala Dinner on December 26, 2026.

offbeat.title: 'Bad Times at the El Royale' bei Pro7:....im TV
offbeat.summary: A TV broadcast of the 2018 film "Bad Times at the El Royale" by Drew Goddard aired on July 23, 2026, on Pro7. The movie is available for streaming on Pro7's online platform, but it will not be rebroadcast on linear TV. The film follows a group of guests with different backgrounds who check into the El Royale hotel, only to discover they are being watched. The guests must navigate their complex situations, leading to a tense and suspenseful outcome.
offbeat.trend: bad times at the el royale

offbeat.title: talkSPORT tips – Best football bets and expert advice for Wednesday 22 July
offbeat.summary: Major League Soccer resumes after a two-month break with several matches on Wednesday, including New England Revolution hosting Toronto. The Revolution are favored to win, with Toronto having kept just two clean sheets all season. The match is part of a busy schedule with qualifiers in European competitions also taking place. The outcome of this match will be a key indicator of Toronto's performance after a nine-match winless run in MLS.

offbeat.title: World Cup 2026: Gavi does not think Paredes should be suspended
offbeat.summary: Spain midfielder Gavi believes Argentina's Leandro Paredes should not be suspended for his role in a confrontation following the World Cup final, where Paredes appeared to push Eric Garcia by the throat. The incident occurred as Spain celebrated their 1-0 win. A disciplinary and ethics prosecutor has been appointed to assess the situation, with potential suspensions and fines possible. The Argentine Football Association may also face a fine.

offbeat.title: Phillies vs. Dodgers Preview: Odds, Pitching Matchup, and Props for July 22
offbeat.summary: The Philadelphia Phillies host the Los Angeles Dodgers at Citizens Bank Park on July 22, 2026, with first pitch at 6:40 p.m. ET. The Phillies aim to bounce back from a 2-1 defeat, seeking to prove their mettle against a top-tier opponent. They trail the Atlanta Braves by three games in the NL East standings, with a 56-46 record. The Dodgers, 64-38, look to build momentum after a 3-2 record in their last five contests.

offbeat.title: Inter Miami vs Chicago Fire live online: score, stats, goals & updates | MLS 2026
offbeat.summary: In a highly contested MLS match, Inter Miami CF took the lead in the 9th minute after a handball by Chicago Fire FC's Anton Salétros. The free kick was awarded to Inter Miami's Sergio Reguilón, who scored the goal. The match is currently underway with Inter Miami holding a 1-0 advantage. The outcome of the game will determine the standings in the MLS 2026 season.

offbeat.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
offbeat.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant in response to ongoing attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz. The potential targets include those located near the capital Tehran. This warning comes after 11 consecutive nights of US strikes on Iran and Iranian state media reports of air defences activated in Tehran. The US has yet to confirm any specific action.

offbeat.title: A year after deadly jet crash at Bangladesh school, families demand answers
offbeat.summary: A year after a Bangladeshi air force jet crashed into a school in Dhaka, killing 36 people, mostly children, families and survivors are still seeking answers. The deadly incident on July 23, 2022, injured over 160 people. Families of the victims gathered at a hospital in Dhaka on Tuesday to mark the anniversary, with many students struggling to return to normal life. The investigation into the crash is ongoing.

breaking.title: Even camels can't cope: Africa's ships of the desert hit by rising temperatures
breaking.summary: Camel herders in Ethiopia’s Afar region report rising temperatures are killing calves and harming adult camels, with one herder losing eight in a month. The extreme heat causes blisters, burns, and hair loss in the animals, which have long been relied on for survival in the arid landscape. Experts warn the trend mirrors broader climate shifts affecting livestock across North and East Africa. Livestock losses are expected to rise as temperatures climb further.

breaking.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
breaking.summary: The US has signed a landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia, allowing the country to enrich uranium on its soil at a US-built facility. The deal, worth billions of dollars to the US, will be used to produce fuel for power plants. US Secretary of Energy Chris Wright said the agreement upholds the highest standards of nuclear safety and nonproliferation. The deal is expected to advance regional security and strengthen the US's competitive edge in civil nuclear technology.

breaking.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
breaking.summary: Ukrainian drones have struck two Russian warehouses belonging to online retailer Wildberries, killing seven workers and injuring dozens in the Tambov region and near Moscow. The attacks, which also damaged goods worth an estimated £750m, are part of Kyiv's campaign to disrupt Russian military supplies. Hundreds of Russian sellers who rely on Wildberries for their livelihoods are now facing financial losses. The strikes mark a new escalation in Ukraine's war against Russia.

facts.title: Most sewage spills in England may be illegal, new research suggests
facts.summary: New research suggests that nearly 9 in 10 hours of raw sewage spills in England may be illegal, with over 86% of incidents occurring during non-heavy rain conditions. The study analyzed data from over 350 storm overflow outlets across nine water companies serving over 30 million people. This finding raises concerns about the legitimacy of sewage spills in England. The water companies will now face scrutiny over their compliance with regulations.

facts.title: What Were the Lotus-Eaters Eating in Homer’s ‘Odyssey’?
facts.summary: In Homer’s epic, Odysseus’s men eat something so powerful that they forget about home. Which plants could have produced such a soporific, narcotic and amnesiac effect?

health.title: Hope for thousands of MS patients as 'life-changing' drug now on NHS in England
health.summary: Up to 5,000 people in England with multiple sclerosis will now benefit from the NHS-approved drug fampridine, which boosts nerve signals to help people walk. The life-changing medication is already available in other parts of the UK. Clinical trials showed nearly 40% of patients experienced improvements in walking speed and duration. This decision could significantly enhance the independence of those affected.

health.title: 1,700 H.I.V. Treatment Sites Closed After Trump Aid Cuts, a Study Finds
health.summary: A study by amfAR found 1,700 H.I.V. treatment sites closed after U.S. funding cuts last year, slashing prevention and care for high-risk groups. Three-quarters of affected organizations stopped serving sex workers, men who have sex with men, transgender people and people who inject drugs. The findings, presented at an AIDS conference in Rio de Janeiro, contradict earlier State Department claims of stable treatment numbers. Global health advocates warn the cuts could reverse years of progress against the disease.

health.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
health.summary: A British health select committee report finds that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a result of personal willpower. The report highlights that ultra-processed foods high in sugar and refined carbohydrates are primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. According to the report, nearly 70% of packaged foods in Britain are ultra-processed. The committee recommends targeting these foods to reduce obesity rates in the country.

crisis.title: Airport drop-off fees up by a third - here are the priciest
crisis.summary: Drop-off fees at the UK's major airports have increased by a third on average since last summer, with two London airports, Gatwick and Stansted, charging the highest fees at £10. This rise comes as Gatwick aims to reduce car usage and increase train and bus arrivals. The fees are part of airports' business models and climate change objectives. The UK's airports now have some of the highest drop-off fees in Europe.

crisis.title: Three Airports Plan to Ditch T.S.A. Amid Push for Private Security
crisis.summary: The airports have opted in to the agency’s new security model, an agency spokesperson confirmed on Wednesday. A union official said the move could jeopardize public safety.

sports.title: McInnes glad of Rangers backing in appeal against ban
sports.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been given a four-match touchline ban by the Scottish FA for criticizing a late penalty decision in a May match against Motherwell. The ban is an immediate three-game suspension with one game pending until the end of the season. McInnes' comments were made when he was in charge of Hearts, and Rangers are now appealing the decision. The case will likely be a key focus in the coming weeks.

sports.title: Tears and sleepless nights as Marshall and Adlington get Scotland flag honour
sports.summary: Scotland’s bowls team will carry the national flag at the Games, led by 59-year-old East Lothian bowler David Marshall and judoka and three-time gold medalist Emma Adlington. Marshall, joined by his niece Beth Riva, called the honour emotional and admitted he nearly cried when told. Adlington, aiming for a third straight gold, revealed the secret had been kept for weeks. The opening ceremony takes place on Friday.

sports.title: Liverpool confident of signing Barcola - Thursday's gossip
sports.summary: Liverpool is confident of signing Paris St-Germain forward Bradley Barcola, a 23-year-old who impressed with four goal contributions at the World Cup with France this summer. The transfer would come after beating Arsenal and Bayern Munich to the signature. Barcola's World Cup performance has boosted Liverpool's chances of securing the young forward. Liverpool will now focus on finalizing the deal.

tech.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
tech.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet reported a negative free cash flow of $5.9 billion for the first time in at least a decade, largely due to spiraling costs of artificial intelligence infrastructure. The company's AI spending is expected to reach $205 billion this year, a significant increase from last year. This surge in spending comes as major tech companies invest heavily in AI technology. Alphabet's stock fell 4% in after-hours trading following the financial report.

tech.title: Meta won’t have to face the next planned social media addiction trial
tech.summary: A 15-year-old plaintiff from Florida has dropped a lawsuit against Meta, just days before a trial was set to begin in a Los Angeles courtroom. The case, which was part of a series of bellwether trials, accused Meta of creating features that harmed and hooked teens. Meta will avoid a potential settlement, following the lead of other social media companies like TikTok, Snap, and YouTube, which previously settled similar claims. The plaintiff will now focus on his recovery and therapy.

tech.title: Alphabet Quadruples Profit to $112 Billion, Fueled by A.I. Investments
tech.summary: Google’s parent company, which has spent heavily on artificial intelligence, also showed growth across its businesses, particularly in cloud computing.

entertainment.title: Yeon Sang-ho Returns to ‘Parasyte’ Universe With ‘Tamiya,’ Starring Shioli Kutsuna, at Netflix
entertainment.summary: South Korean filmmaker Yeon Sang-ho is returning to the "Parasyte" universe with a new live-action series, "Parasyte: Tamiya," starring Shioli Kutsuna, set to stream on Netflix in 2027. The series marks a collaboration between Netflix and Yeon, who developed the show and co-wrote the script with Ryu Yong-jae. This is Yeon's second project with Netflix, following "Hellbound." The series is expected to premiere next year.

entertainment.title: Shaken, Soaked and Seasick: What It’s Like to Survive ‘The Odyssey’ in 4DX
entertainment.summary: A new immersive screening of Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey in 4DX adds seat rumbling, wind, and water effects to the three-hour epic. The experience, available at select theaters, targets fans seeking heightened sensory thrills beyond standard formats. Showtimes begin this weekend, offering a fresh way to experience the Matt Damon-starring adventure.

entertainment.title: ‘Doctor Strange’ Director Says Elon Musk ‘Doesn’t Know S— About Cinema’ After Musk Vows to Make AI ‘Odyssey’: ‘His Opinions on Art Are Worthless’
entertainment.summary: Elon Musk vowed on July 22, 2026, to use his AI tool Grok to produce a full-length, historically accurate film adaptation of Homer’s The Odyssey by year’s end. Director Scott Derrickson, known for Doctor Strange, publicly dismissed Musk’s claim, calling him uninformed about cinema and art. Musk had previously criticized Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey over casting choices. The debate highlights tensions between AI-driven filmmaking and traditional Hollywood perspectives.

politics.title: With Saudi Deal, Trump Loosens Nuclear Standards for One Close Ally
politics.summary: Nuclear experts say it throws fuel on the smoldering fire of atomic proliferation, creating a destabilizing situation that could raise the risk of nuclear war.

politics.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
politics.summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.
